name: Russia (1858–1896)

colors.symbolism: added to distinguish from the Austrian black-yellow bicolour

description: A horizontal tricolour of black, yellow (orange-gold) and white bands of equal width — the Imperial livery colours.
history: Declared in 1858 by Alexander II as the national flag of the Russian Empire, recognised as its first officially approved state flag. It was supplanted by the white-blue-red tricolour, which became the sole national flag by 1896.
